Product.title: Product

id.description: Unique identifier for the product entity.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the authenticated user can read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores products generated by the user.  Only the authenticated user can list, read, create, update, and delete their own products.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the WooSenteur AI application, focusing on secure user data management and efficient product description generation. It enforces Authorization Independence by structuring product data within user-specific subcollections, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. 

The primary collections are `/users/{userId}` for user profiles and `/users/{userId}/products/{productId}` for products generated by each user. This hierarchical structure ensures that users can only access their own data. The structure is designed to minimize authorization complexity, enhance debuggability, and prevent unauthorized data access.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** The data structure avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies. All products are stored under user-specific subcollections, allowing security rules to authorize access based on the request's authenticated `uid` and the path, without needing to `get()` parent document data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The use of subcollections enables secure list operations. Security rules can easily restrict listing, reading, creating, updating, and deleting products to the owner (the user identified by `{userId}` in the path). This prevents unauthorized listing of all products across different users.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid` and path-based ownership. No custom claims are used.

**Invariants:** Path-based ownership supports the integrity of ownership, ensuring that each product is associated with its owner.
